title: Creating & Managing Deals
description: Learn how to create new deals, assign team members, update deal status, switch between grid and table views, and filter your deal pipeline.

steps.narration: Your deals pipeline is the command center for all active engagements. Here you can see seven deals at a glance, each showing the company, status, and owner.
Let's create a new deal. Click the New Deal button in the upper right to open the deal creation form.
Fill in the deal details. We'll name this one Cloud Migration Assessment and associate it with Meridian Dynamics since we're expanding our engagement with them.

actions.selector: input[name='name'], input[placeholder*='deal name' i], input[placeholder*='Deal name' i]

steps.narration: After submitting, the new deal appears in your grid right away. AtlusDeck automatically sets the status to open and assigns you as the owner.

callout.text: New deal added to your pipeline

steps.narration: Click any deal card to open its overview page. Let's look at the Meridian Dynamics Q2 Digital Transformation Initiative to see the full deal context.
Back on the deals page, let's manage team assignments. Click the assign button on the Meridian deal to add a team member.
Select a team member from the dropdown. Assigning deals helps distribute workload and keeps the right people connected to each opportunity.
Now let's update a deal status. Changing the Meridian deal from open to won marks it as a successfully closed engagement.

callout.text: Update deal status to track progress

steps.narration: Switch to table view for a more compact layout. The toggle in the upper right lets you choose between the visual grid and a traditional table format.

actions.selector: button[aria-label*='table' i], button[aria-label*='Table' i], button[data-testid='view-toggle-table'], [role='tablist'] button:nth-child(2)

steps.narration: Table view shows all your deals in a scannable list with sortable columns. This is ideal when you need to compare details across many deals quickly.
Use the status filter to narrow your view. Let's filter to show only won deals so we can see which engagements have closed successfully.
With the filter active, only your won deals are visible. Clear the filter anytime to see the full pipeline again.
